The left atrium and right atrium are normal in cavity size. The right atrial pressure is indeterminate.

Apical function is preserved ([**Last Name (un) 13367**] sign).
The aortic valve leaflets (3) appear structurally normal with good leaflet excursion and no aortic stenosis. Trace aortic regurgitation is seen.

Right ventricular cavity dilation with free wall hypokinesis. Mild global left ventricular hypokinesis c/w diffuse process (toxin, metabolic, etc.).

While positioning differences could contribute to loss of visualization of some lower lobe branch pulmonary vessels, propagation of PE must be strongly considered
There are no new focal consolidations and the CP angles remain sharp.
FINDINGS: As compared to the previous radiograph, there is unchanged right mediastinal widening and increased diameter of the pulmonary arteries.
